description: Where an arm meets the torso, 'talua' is both the joint and its surrounding region. It adjoins the chest and back without covering either one. A physical load may rest on a shoulder; an abstract demand or responsibility is 'tupeka' (burden) rather than a hidden sense of the body-part word.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into open central unrounded 'a'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and raise the back of the tongue into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Keep the final open central 'a' in its own syllable across the hiatus.

semantic_domains.physical: the joint and region where an arm meets the torso
